channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ovechkin leads entire Capitals team in tribute to Wild's Fleury with special postgame handshake line
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Alex Ovechkin didn't score for Washington, staying at 889 career goals and six away from breaking Wayne Gretzky's all-time record. Ovechkin did initiate a memorable moment on the ice in Minnesota on Thursday night, though.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Josh Giddey's halfcourt heave at the buzzer gives Bulls 119-117 win over the Lakers
item.description: Josh Giddey made a halfcourt heave at the buzzer to cap perhaps the wildest finish in the NBA this season, giving the Chicago Bulls a 119-117 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Seligman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dodgers celebrate 2024 World Series victory with Ice Cube and Kirk Gibson at home opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It’s a new season but the Los Angeles Dodgers are still celebrating their 2024 World Series championship. Rapper Ice Cube rolled up to their dugout in a vintage blue convertible with the Commissioner’s Trophy in the front passenger seat.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Novak Djokovic beats Sebastian Korda, advances to semifinals in push for 7th Miami Open title
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Novak Djokovic is finding a higher gear in South Florida after a sluggish start to 2025.
item.dc:creator: MARC BERMAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Led by Mark Sears, Alabama breaks the record for 3-pointers in a March Madness game with 25
item.description: Led by Mark Sears, Alabama broke the 35-year-old record for 3-pointers in an NCAA Tournament game, making 25 in a 113-88 victory over BYU in an East Region semifinal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scottie Scheffler 2 back of a 4-way tie for the lead at Houston Open. Rory McIlroy 5 behind
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Scottie Scheffler and Rory McIlroy wanted a final tune-up before the Masters and spent parts of the Thursday at the Houston Open under an umbrella in wind and rain that prevented anyone from getting too far away.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Devin Williams saves first game with New York Yankees by a whisker
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Devin Williams saved his first game with the New York Yankees by a whisker. Acquired from Milwaukee in December, the bearded All-Star closer drew his first boos from Yankees fans just 18 pitches into his career in pinstripes. “I heard ‘em. I heard ’em,” he said.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bryce Harper hears boos again at Nationals Park and homers again on opening day for the Phillies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The jeers greeted the announcement of Bryce Harper's name during pregame introductions at Nationals Park on Thursday. And when he stepped to the plate in the top of the first inning. Again in the fourth.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Broadway actress faces criticism for reading national anthem lyrics from phone during performance before game
item.description: Betsy Wolfe was tapped to sing the national anthem before the New York Yankees' opening-day game but a quirk in her performance raised eyebrows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Padres rally in seventh inning to win home opener against Braves
item.description: The Padres kicked off the 2025-2026 season with a comeback victory over the Atlanta Braves Thursday at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: White Sox hammer Angels in lopsided Opening Day win
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The weather on White Sox Opening Day mirrored their franchise outlook heading into 2025. Murky, gray and dreary with little to be excited about besides the fact baseball season is all the way back. Good thing the White Sox played well in spite of the melancholy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Auburn's Pearl says his March Madness call for hostage release was about 'survival,' not politics
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Auburn coach Bruce Pearl says he wasn't mixing politics with sports last week when he called for the release of an American hostage in Gaza. “It’s not political. It’s survival,” Pearl told The Associated Press on Thursday following his news conference ahead of No.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State audit reveals Anaheim doesn’t know condition of Angel Stadium on Opening Day
item.description: A report from the California State Auditor found that the city of Anaheim has not been able to ensure that Angel Stadium, one of the oldest stadiums in the majors, is being properly maintained.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: SF's Chase Center hosting NCAA Men's Basketball games Thursday, Saturday
item.description: (BCN) -- San Francisco is hosting two games at Chase Center on Thursday in the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship tournament. At 4:39 p.m., University of Florida, which is the No. 1 seed in the West region of the tournament's bracket, plays No. 4 University of Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: White Sox have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: Florida coach brings team to famed local staple ahead of March Madness matchup in SF
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Florida head coach Todd Golden is excited to be back in San Francisco this week -- a city where he began his head coaching career. Golden leads the No. 1 seed Gators for their Sweet 16 matchup Thursday against No. 4 seed Maryland.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Baseball: Southwest 7, Crawford 0
item.description: Baseball time on Tuesday afternoon with Crawford hosting Southwest. The Raiders won 7-0. Sebestian Martinez was stellar on the bump for the Raiders complete game shutout scattering five hits with 12 strikeouts.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Warriors' Stephen Curry won't play in Miami, 2nd straight game missed with pelvic contusion
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Stephen Curry is going to take a little more time to heal. Curry remained out of Golden State's lineup Tuesday night for a game against the Miami Heat, the second straight game he has missed with a pelvic contusion.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bryan Hodgson named University of South Florida head men's basketball coach
item.description: Bryan Hodgson, one of the nation’s top recruiters and a rising head coach, has been named the University of South Florida head men’s basketball coach, Vice President for Athletics Michael Kelly announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Merissa Lynn, USF Athletics, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stephen Curry listed as questionable for Golden State's Tuesday game in Miami
item.description: Stephen Curry is back with the Golden State Warriors after missing the first game of the team's road trip with a pelvic contusion, and could play when his team takes on the Miami Heat in Jimmy Butler's return game Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms relationship with Vanessa Trump
item.description: “Love is in the air and life is better with you by my side! We look forward to our journey through life together,” Woods said on X, where he has 6.4 million followers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: McNeese's Will Wade signs a 6-year deal to become NC State coach, AP source says
item.description: McNeese coach Will Wade has a signed six-year deal to take over as N.C. State's next coach, a person with knowledge of the situation told The Associated Press on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lakers play the Bulls on 3-game win streak
item.description: Chicago Bulls (30-40, ninth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Los Angeles Lakers (43-25, fourth in the Western Conference) Los Angeles; Saturday, 10:30 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: Los Angeles heads into a matchup with Chicago as winners of three games in a row. The Lakers are 28-7 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vancouver Whitecaps bring shutout streak into matchup with the Chicago Fire
item.description: Chicago Fire (2-1-1) vs. Vancouver Whitecaps FC (4-0-0) Vancouver, British Columbia; Saturday, 10:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Oakland Roots sell over 24K tickets to Coliseum home opener
item.description: (KRON) -- The Oakland Roots Soccer Club have sold over 24,000 tickets to their home opener on Saturday. The team, which will make its debut Saturday at the iconic Oakland Coliseum, is already set to smash its previous attendance record.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4
